title: docs: how a downstream repo integrates assay into CI
description: README needs a 'Using assay in your CI' section so another repo can adopt the drift-check pattern: install assay, add map/map:check Taskfile targets, add a GHA workflow that runs the drift-check, commit a snapshot baseline. Use assay's own dogfooded setup (.github/workflows/assay-map.yml + Taskfile map/map:check + task ci pre-push) as the reference. Note single-repo (scan .) in CI vs cross-repo (needs multiple checkouts).

title: extract/cargo: Rust crate dependency extractor (cross-repo path/git/registry deps)
description: v2 extractor. Add `extract/cargo` behind the existing internal/extract.Extractor interface, mirroring extract/gomod: parse Cargo.toml (incl. workspace + member crates) and emit identity-keyed producer/consumer facts with provenance — package declaration (producer, a crate/cargo-kind identity) + each dependency (consumer): path deps (`path = '../X'` = local cross-repo link), git deps (`git = '...agentic-research/...'` = cross-repo), and registry deps. This is the gap dogfooding exposed: `assay map rosary mache ley-line-open cloister` finds only mache->ley-line-open (go_module) and MISSES cloister->ley-line-open (real Cargo dep: cloister/rs/crates/cas pins leyline-cas-ffi) plus the rosary/ley-line Rust edges — because there's no Cargo extractor. PREREQUISITE: confirm via the dedup check that mache/ley-line-open don't already expose crate-dependency edges to consume (manifest-level cross-repo deps appear to be assay's niche, but verify). New Kind (cargo_crate) in internal/artifact; register in cmd/map.go; TDD with fixtures modeling cloister's path/git dep on ley-line-open. Then re-run the 4-repo validation and confirm cloister->ley-line-open now resolves.

comments.text:
Implemented in worktree assay-cargo (branch feat-cargo-extractor), commit 6152206. NOT merged, NOT closed.

What landed:
- internal/artifact/kind.go: new Kind `KindCargoCrate` ("cargo_crate"). Crate identity = crate name; version requirements are never part of identity (nothing to strip — facts carry the bare name).
- internal/extract/cargo/{cargo.go,manifest.go}: Extractor mirroring gomod. Walks a root for every Cargo.toml (skips .git/.claude/target/node_modules/vendor/testdata), so workspace members are discovered by walking — virtual [workspace] roots contribute no producer. Producers: [package].name. Consumers: every dependency table — [dependencies]/[dev-dependencies]/[build-dependencies], their [workspace.*] and [target.<cfg>.*] variants, and [deps.<crate>] subtables. Dependencies emitted by CRATE NAME (the `package = ` rename when present, else the table key) so path/git/registry deps match a producer of the same name across roots. Parsed via go-toml/v2 unstable AST (added dep; GOWORK=off go mod tidy) for crate names + line provenance; subtable deps anchor to their first key line. Consumers deduped per-manifest by crate name. Always Available() (pure file parsing); per-file parse errors return as soft Failed records.
- cmd/map.go: registered cargo.New() alongside gomod/dockerfile/ci/gocode.

Tests: internal/extract/cargo/cargo_test.go + testdata/ fixtures (producer root = ley-line-open shape with workspace members producing leyline-cas-ffi + leyline-core; consumer root = cloister shape with git dep on leyline-cas-ffi (package rename) + path dep + dev-dep; forms fixture for string/inline/subtable-rename/target/build forms; target/ skip). title: CI: `assay map` GitHub Action — emit + drift-check the dependency graph
description: v2. Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs `assay map` in CI so the derived dependency/seam graph stays current and never drifts. Two modes worth supporting: (1) regenerate the mermaid/JSON map as an artifact (or commit it / update a docs file), and (2) DRIFT-CHECK: compare freshly-derived map to a committed snapshot and fail if they differ (so a new undeclared cross-repo seam shows up in CI). This also dogfoods the `ci` extractor. Follow the taskfile-ci-parity pattern: a Taskfile target (e.g. `task map` / `task map:check`) that the GHA INVOKES, rather than re-implementing the command in YAML — so local == CI. Scope note: single-repo in-CI (one scan root = the checked-out repo) is the natural first cut; cross-repo CI needs multiple checkouts (future). Files: .github/workflows/assay-map.yml + a Taskfile target.

comments.text: Implemented in worktree (branch feat-assay-map-gha), commit a4ec62c. Verified: map:check passes clean (exit 0) and fails on an injected import (net/http/httptest -> exit 1), then reverted clean. Cross-repo multi-checkout noted as future scope. title: extract/capnp: cloister service-topology edges from capnp const data
description:
Confirmed-viable follow-on from assay-1d319b. cloister declares its runtime service topology as capnp CONST DATA (not just schema), so it's deterministically extractable: cluster.capnp (1 const — notme-identity with image notme:0.1.0, mache), config.capnp (2 consts — mache-mcp, rosary-mcp, the notme /identity proxy binding). manifest/cluster.capnp is SCHEMA (29 struct defs) — skip it.

Add `extract/capnp` behind internal/extract.Extractor: parse the const data instances and emit KindService (added by the wrangler extractor — reuse it) producer/consumer facts → cloister->mache (mache-mcp), cloister->rosary (rosary-mcp), cloister->notme (identity proxy). Approach: parse the capnp const text directly, OR shell to `capnp eval`/`capnp compile -o json` if the capnp CLI is present (deterministic, structured) with a text fallback. Register in cmd/map.go; TDD with fixtures derived from cluster.capnp/config.capnp.

SUCCESS: `assay map cloister mache rosary notme` surfaces the MCP/proxy edges the wiki's CONCEPTUAL graph currently carries by hand → closes the gap between the generated build graph and the hand-maintained runtime graph (at which point the hand-drawn mermaid can retire). This is the answer to 'shouldn't runtime edges be auto?' — yes, because they're declared as structured data.

comments.text:
Implemented internal/extract/capnp (commit 04c4bcc on branch feat-capnp-extractor, worktree assay-capnp). Mirrors wrangler/cargo.

Parse approach: TEXT parser over const-data records, NOT the capnp CLI (CLI would need every imported schema to resolve per scan root — brittle across a multi-repo scan, and unavailable without the toolchain). The ( key = value, ... ) records parse deterministically from file bytes with exact file+line provenance. hasConst gates files: a pure-schema .capnp (struct/interface/annotation, no const) yields zero facts (no false positives from struct field names).

Honest, data-driven kind derivation (no name table, no cross-kind match):
- service binding (service = "X") / worker-fronting config service entry -> KindService
- external bundle image = "X" -> KindContainerImage using the declared ref

Proof (GOWORK=off go run . map cloister mache rosary notme --format mermaid --group repo): cloister->notme edge went 1 -> 2 artifacts (capnp's config.capnp NOTME->notme-bot service binding resolves to notme's published service:notme-bot). cloister->mache and cloister->rosary correctly fall to EXTERNAL and are surfaced as true findings: cluster.capnp references them by local image tags (mache:0.8.0, rosary:0.2.0) that don't match published refs, and config.capnp references them by MCP service names (mache-mcp, rosary-mcp) nothing publishes. Confirmed via --format md external section, all with capnp provenance.

ECOSYSTEM FIX (separate bead, NOT an assay hack): making cloister->mache / ->rosary RESOLVE requires mache/rosary to declare a service identity, OR cloister to reference the published image ref (e.g. ghcr.io/agentic-research/rosary instead of bare rosary:0.2.0). The asymmetry (notme declares its service; mache/rosary don't) is the valuable signal the tool reveals.

Verification (all GOWORK=off): gofumpt -l -extra . clean; go vet clean; golangci-lint 0 issues; go test -race ./... all pass (incl. new internal/extract/capnp). task map regenerated docs/dependency-graph.md (committed). task ci exits 0. No new deps (go mod tidy no-op).

Registered in cmd/map.go. Not closing, not merging per instructions.

title: extract/service: runtime/service-topology edges from structured config (capnp manifests + wrangler bindings)
description:
v2/v3 extractor. The runtime/MCP/proxy edges (rosary->mache over MCP, cloister->notme proxy, cloister->mache/rosary MCP backends) are NOT in the universal manifests assay reads today (go.mod/Cargo.toml/Docker/CI) — but in THIS ecosystem they ARE declared in structured config, so they're derivable, just from different formats:
- cloister capnp SERVICE MANIFESTS: cluster.capnp / config.capnp / manifest/cluster.capnp reference mache/rosary/notme/ghcr.io (the declared service topology). Target the config INSTANCES (declared bindings), not the .capnp SCHEMA type defs.
- cloister wrangler.toml: Cloudflare service bindings (which services/workers it binds to).
These map to new producer/consumer facts: a service/endpoint Kind (e.g. KindService / KindEndpoint), edges keyed on service identity so they resolve cross-root like the others.

Add `extract/service` (or per-format: extract/capnp + extract/wrangler) behind internal/extract.Extractor, mirroring the existing extractors. New Kind in internal/artifact; register in cmd/map.go; TDD with fixtures modeling cloister's cluster.capnp + wrangler bindings. SUCCESS: `assay map` over cloister/mache/rosary/notme surfaces the MCP/proxy edges that the wiki's conceptual graph currently carries by hand — closing the gap between the GENERATED build graph and the hand-maintained runtime graph.

OUT OF SCOPE (harder, separate): edges encoded purely in code (hardcoded socket paths like default.sock / LEYLINE_SOCKET with no structured declaration) — those need convention-scanning, not a parser; note them as dangling/external rather than fabricating.

comments.text: WRANGLER HALF DONE + MERGED to main (4ae0a93). extract/wrangler parses wrangler.toml service bindings → KindService producer/consumer facts; PROOF: `assay map cloister notme` now shows cloister→notme (1 artifact, the /identity service binding) — a runtime edge invisible before. 15 pkgs green, lint 0. title: Build the conversation-relative recall validation harness (Recall@k/MRR vs BM25 baseline)
description: Validation harness for the conversation-relative recall hypothesis (see art-wiki docs/problems/selective-recall.md — full validation plan + first N=1 probe showing the effect direction holds). Information-retrieval ablation: hold corpus + probe-points + gold fixed; swap only the scoring function — (A) static semantic search [baseline], (B) live-conversation similarity (rolling context as query), (C) HDC dual-readout. Compute Recall@k / MRR / nDCG@k; report latency + tokens. Gold labels via distant supervision harvested from lectio provenance (turns that rediscovered an item already in the store — e.g. the ADR-0018 episode) plus public benchmarks (LoCoMo, LongMemEval) for comparability with mem0/Letta/Zep. HARD GATE: must beat plain BM25 first; lock probe set + thresholds ex-ante; held-out data. Two layers: intrinsic (HDC representation — STS-B/symbolic recovery, per ley-line HDC decade) vs extrinsic (recall improvement). NOTE: harness code likely lives in lectio (~/github/jamestexas/lectio) and/or ley-line-open; tracked here because it validates dk6.4 (MiniLM vs HDC matcher). HDC leg (C) is gated on ley-line's hdc-translate (Proposed); A-vs-B is runnable today against lectio's MCP. Refs: docs/prior-art/agent-memory-systems.md; ley-line-open/docs/decades/2026-hdc-translation-layer.md.

title: Decision: mache coupling — read .db canonical views vs subprocess/MCP
description:
[T0-1] Resolve open question #1. mache exposes its symbol surface via (a) canonical SQLite views v_defs/v_refs in a .db (EnsureCanonicalViews installs them idempotently for external openers; frozen by ADR-0013), (b) MCP/HTTP/stdio serve, (c) mache pack. Decide which boundary extract/gocode's mache backend targets. Recommended to evaluate: target .db + canonical views as primary (deterministic, process-free, ADR-0013-versioned), stdio/HTTP as fallback for un-parsed live sources.

Acceptance: a written decision states the primary coupling mechanism (.db views vs subprocess), the exact contract to read (v_defs, v_refs), the Go SQLite driver choice (e.g. modernc.org/sqlite), the fallback when no .db exists, and whether mache must be running. Referenced by T3-2b.

comments.text: Decision (docs/decisions/0001-mache-coupling.md, branch assay-design-decisions, commit e09bf53): extract/gocode reads mache's on-disk .db canonical views v_defs/v_refs, opened READ-ONLY via the pure-Go modernc.org/sqlite driver ("sqlite", no cgo). mache does NOT need to be running. assay obtains a .db either supplied or via `mache build <root> <tmp.db>` (one-shot build step, not a live dependency), calls EnsureCanonicalViews (mache exports CanonicalViewsDDL + EnsureCanonicalViews for exactly this consumer case) to tolerate older .dbs, then queries v_defs(token,node_id,fidelity) / v_refs(referrer_node_id,token,target_node_id,ref_uri,ref_line,fidelity), joining nodes + _ast for file:line provenance. stdio/MCP is fallback ONLY (no mache binary, or un-parsed live sources). Grounded in mache: go.mod:22 modernc.org/sqlite v1.52.0; sqlite_writer.go:118-128 view defs, :248 read-only LoadFileIndex pattern assay copies, :292-328 exported DDL/EnsureCanonicalViews; ADR-0013 makes the views the canonical consumer surface (forward-compatible with LSP binding rows via UNION ALL, consumer SQL unchanged). Residual risk: views are ADR-0013 "Proposed" (pin a fixture .db + golden test); pin --backend=tree-sitter for deterministic byte ranges. Left open for human review.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: Decision: identity normalization rules for image & module refs
description:
[T0-3] Resolve open question #4. Decide how aggressively to canonicalize artifact identities so a producer id matches a consumer reference: container image refs (registry/tag/digest so A's published ghcr tag matches B's FROM), Go module identity incl. pseudo-versions and subdirectory module paths (e.g. ley-line-open/clients/go/leyline-schema where repo identity != module path), and CI env interpolation (github.repository_owner -> agentic-research). Output: a normalization spec the resolver and extractors share. READY (independent of the Go type defs in T1-1; canonical string forms).

Acceptance: written spec defines canonical forms for container image refs (tag vs digest vs implicit :latest, registry host defaults), Go module ids (pseudo-version stripping, subdir-path -> owning-repo mapping), and CI-expanded image coordinates, with worked examples for ghcr.io/agentic-research/rosary and github.com/agentic-research/mache. Referenced by T4-1 and the T3 extractors.

comments.text: Decision (docs/decisions/0002-identity-normalization.md, branch assay-design-decisions, commit e09bf53): each artifact kind gets Canonicalize(raw,ctx) producing two keys — an IdentityKey (version-stripped, what the resolver matches on) and a StrictKey (with tag/digest/module-version). Two passes: (1) interpolation (resolve CI ${{...}} vars from per-root context: github.repository_owner -> origin owner = agentic-research, env.* from the workflow's own env: block), then (2) normalization. Image refs: default registry docker.io (bare name -> docker.io/library/name), implicit :latest, digest beats tag, identity key = registry/repository. Go modules: strip version incl. pseudo-versions; keep /v2 major suffix; replace directives key on the left (original) path; subdir module path != owning repo so emit BOTH module identity (full path) and owning-repo identity (host/owner/repo, first 3 segments). Resolver groups by (Kind,IdentityKey); records version_match in {exact,mismatch,unknown}; buckets resolved/external/dangling. WORKED: ghcr.io/${{github.repository_owner}}/rosary -> ghcr.io/agentic-research/rosary (identity key) matches a FROM ...:latest consumer = one resolved cross-root edge, version_match=mismatch (v0.1.0 vs latest). github.com/agentic-research/mache (mache go.mod) matches x-ray's `require ...mache v0.5.5` (go.mod:6) after stripping v0.5.5; commented replace =>../mache (go.mod:51) still keys on the original path. Subdir: ley-line-open/clients/go/leyline-schema -> owning repo github.com/agentic-research/ley-line-open. Grounded in rosary release.yml:21, x-ray go.mod + Dockerfile (golang:1.26 -> docker.io/library/golang external; gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12 external), ley-line-open subdir go.mod. Main tunable flagged: resolve on identity key (max seam discovery) and surface version mismatches as weak edges rather than misses. Left open for human review.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: Decision: drift fallback in v1 or deferred
description:
[T0-4] Resolve open question #3. Decide whether `assay drift <root...> --against <doc>` ships in v1 (thin: does ARCHITECTURE.md mention each resolved seam?) or defers to v2. Output: a go/no-go that gates the T6 thread. If deferred, T6 beads are parked, not built.

Acceptance: a written go/no-go states whether drift is in v1, and if so its exact thin scope (input doc format, what 'mentions a seam' means, output). Referenced by T6-1.

comments.text: Decision (docs/decisions/0003-drift-v1-scope.md, branch assay-design-decisions, commit e09bf53): DEFER the drift command to v2. v1 ships derive-and-emit ONLY (assay map <root...> -> resolved/external/dangling graph as JSON + Markdown/mermaid). No `assay drift --against <doc>` in v1. This is a go/no-go that PARKS the T6 thread (not cancels) — confirms the lead's YAGNI preference. Rationale: spec already subordinates drift ("derive, not grade"; grading is "a fallback, not the main act"; v1-scope offers it as "thin v1 or deferred"); drift is strictly downstream of a stable map; the headline value (cross-root build-artifact edges, image-built-in-A-consumed-in-B) lives entirely in the map; a "thin" drift regresses toward the discredited |C∩D|/|C| presence metric, and doing it well drags in the parked dk6.4 semantic matching. Interim substitute needing no hand-written doc: the dangling (unused producer) and external (outside-world dependency) buckets answer most of "what's undocumented/unused" for free. v2 T6 contract recorded against a frozen, golden-tested map JSON schema (reuse dk6.1 ClaimSource + internal/docs markdown parsing; buckets covered/missing/phantom). PRODUCT-SCOPE CALL — flagged for human confirmation: (1) v1 = map only [recommended]; (2) accept "is ARCHITECTURE.md still true?" is a v2 answer; (3) if rejected, adopt ONLY the thin literal-id-presence drift scoped in the ADR, nothing larger. Left open for human review.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: Define internal/artifact vocabulary (Identity, Artifact, Producer, Consumer, Edge, kinds)
description:
[T1-1] Create internal/artifact: the Kind enum (GoModule, GoPackageSymbol, ContainerImage, CLIBinary; extensible), a stable global Identity type (canonical string keyed by kind), Artifact, Provenance (file path + line), Producer and Consumer records (Identity + Provenance + kind), and the directed Edge (Producer->Consumer). Greenfield. Repo-agnostic foundation: resolution keys on Identity, not location. Pure types + equality/canonical-key helpers; no I/O, no parsing.

Acceptance: internal/artifact compiles; Identity/Artifact/Kind/Provenance/Producer/Consumer/Edge exist; Identity has a canonical-key method + value equality; tests assert equal Identities for equivalent ids and distinct for differing kinds; no imports of internal/code, internal/coverage, or any extractor.

comments.text:
T1-1 internal/artifact vocabulary done on branch assay-T1-artifact (commit 2f4c81b). Pure types, no I/O, no deps on internal/code or internal/coverage.

Types:
- Kind (string-backed, extensible): KindGoModule/GoPackageSymbol/ContainerImage/CLIBinary; .String(), .Valid() against validKinds set.
- Identity{Kind, Ref} — comparable value (== and map-key usable). Canonical-key method: Key() = Kind.String() + "\x00" + Ref. NUL separator => a Ref can't forge another kind's key; same Ref under different Kind yields distinct keys. .Equal(other) (==), .IsZero(). NewIdentity(kind, ref) trims surrounding whitespace ONLY.
- Provenance{File, Line}; Artifact{Identity, Name}; Producer{Identity, Provenance}+Kind(); Consumer{Identity, Provenance}+Kind(); Edge{Producer, Consumer}.

Reviewer note: NewIdentity does trivial trim only; code comments defer formal normalization policy to T0-3 (assay-926eb8) — kept pluggable, nothing hardcoded.

Files: internal/artifact/{kind,identity,artifact}.go + artifact_test.go.
GOWORK=off: gofumpt -l internal/artifact/ clean; go vet clean; go test -race ./internal/artifact/... => ok. Not closing; left for review. Not merged.

description:
[T0-2] Resolve open question #2. Decide whether internal/graph serializes to a bespoke JSON schema or a shape already consumable by mache/a diagram tool. Output: a documented JSON schema (artifact ids, edges, bucket labels resolved/external/dangling, provenance) implemented by T4-2 and T5-1; stable for golden tests (byte-deterministic ordering).

Acceptance: a written schema enumerates the JSON fields for artifacts/edges/buckets/provenance, specifies deterministic ordering rules, and notes whether it targets an existing ingest format. Referenced by T4-2 and T5-1.

title: internal/resolve: global identity matching into resolved/external/dangling buckets
description:
[T4-1] Implement internal/resolve: a single global pass over all producer/consumer facts from all roots that joins consumer references to producer ids by artifact kind, keying on normalized Identity (T0-3) so repo boundaries are invisible. Three COMPUTED buckets: resolved (consumer ref matches a producer in some scanned root), external (no scanned producer — real outside-world dep), dangling (producer no consumer references — candidate dead surface). external-vs-internal is computed by whether a scanned root produces the id, never configured, never a module-path-prefix heuristic. Deterministic ordering.

Acceptance: unit tests cover all three buckets incl. a multi-root producer-in-A-consumed-in-B resolved edge; matching uses normalized identity (T0-3); order-deterministic; no config toggles internal/external.

title: Update CLAUDE.md and README.md: derive-not-grade vocabulary/framing
description:
[T7-1] Rewrite the framing/vocabulary sections of assay's CLAUDE.md and README.md to replace the 'documentation coverage via set operations' / |C ∩ D| / |C| framing with the deterministic artifact/usage graph MODEL: derive-not-grade arrow, scan-roots (repo-agnostic), the Artifact/Extractor/Resolver/Map vocabulary, and the resolved/external/dangling buckets. Record the parked non-goals so they are not silently reintroduced. SCOPE: concepts + parked non-goals only — the concrete command surface (assay map flags) and the extractors->resolver->graph->map diagram are documented in T7-3 once they ship, to avoid documenting un-built behavior.

Acceptance: CLAUDE.md and README.md describe the artifact/usage-graph model and vocabulary, no longer present set-coverage as the product, list parked non-goals, and reference the spec; they do NOT yet assert a finished assay map flag surface.

title: extract/gomod: module decl + require + replace edges
description:
[T3-1] Implement the gomod extractor behind internal/extract.Extractor. Producers: the module declaration (Go module Identity from the `module` line). Consumers: each `require github.com/...` AND each `replace ... => ../<repo>` including COMMENTED replace directives (the commented `// replace github.com/agentic-research/mache => ../mache` encodes the local-dev cross-root link). external-vs-internal is NOT decided here — emit every require/replace as a consumer fact keyed on normalized Identity; the resolver decides. Handle pseudo-versions and subdir module paths per T0-3. Provenance = go.mod path + line.

Acceptance: given a fixture go.mod with module decl, requires, an active replace, and a commented replace, the extractor emits the module producer + a consumer per require and per replace (active and commented) with line provenance; pseudo-version + subdir-path module paths normalize per T0-3; a fixture modeling x-ray/go.mod's require of github.com/agentic-research/mache yields the expected cross-repo consumer record.

title: extract/dockerfile: FROM, stages, COPY --from, published tags
description:
[T3-3] Implement the dockerfile extractor behind internal/extract.Extractor. Producers: container-image Identities for build targets / published tags (stages named via AS). Consumers: each FROM base image and each COPY --from=<stage|image>. Must resolve multi-stage `AS <alias>` so COPY --from=builder maps to the right stage (internal edge, not external image), and handle ARG-interpolated platforms ($BUILDPLATFORM) + ARG substitution. Real reference: x-ray/Dockerfile (multi-stage golang builder -> gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12, COPY --from=builder). Image identities normalized per T0-3. Provenance = Dockerfile path + line.

Acceptance: given a fixture multi-stage Dockerfile (named stages, external FROM base, COPY --from=builder, ARG platform), emits image-base consumers, resolves COPY --from stage aliases to internal stages (not external images), emits the build-target producer; image refs normalize per T0-3; an x-ray/Dockerfile-modeled fixture yields the expected external-base consumer records with line provenance.

title: extract/ci: GitHub Actions image build/publish/pull/run signals
description:
[T3-4] Implement the GitHub Actions CI extractor behind internal/extract.Extractor. Producers: container images a workflow builds/publishes — critically, image coordinates declared in `env:` with ${{ github.* }} interpolation (rosary/.github/workflows/release.yml: `IMAGE_NAME: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository_owner }}/rosary`), expanding github.repository_owner -> agentic-research per T0-3. Consumers: jobs that pull images or run binaries. Do NOT assume docker/build-push-action is present (none exists in these repos) — read env + steps generically. Provenance = workflow path + line.

Acceptance: given a fixture workflow with env IMAGE_NAME using ${{ github.repository_owner }}, emits a published-image producer with owner expanded per T0-3 + line provenance; a step running a binary emits a CLI/binary consumer; no dependency on docker/build-push-action; a rosary/release.yml-modeled fixture yields the expected ghcr.io/agentic-research/rosary producer.

title: internal/graph: resolved graph model + serialization
description:
[T4-2] Implement internal/graph: an in-memory graph of Artifacts (nodes) and Edges (resolved/external/dangling-tagged), plus serialization to the JSON schema decided in T0-2, with deterministic ordering for golden tests. Construct the graph from internal/resolve output. Round-trip (serialize -> deserialize) preserves the graph. No rendering here (Markdown/mermaid is T5).

Acceptance: given a resolved bucket set, internal/graph builds a graph and serializes to the T0-2 JSON schema; serialization is byte-deterministic for identical input; a round-trip test reconstructs an equal graph; bucket labels + provenance survive serialization.

title: extract/gocode: mache .db backend (v_defs/v_refs) selected when present
description:
[T3-2b] Add the mache .db backend to extract/gocode behind the T3-2a shell, building on the dk6.3 MacheVerifier. Per the T0-1 decision, read mache's canonical .db views (v_defs/v_refs via the chosen SQLite driver) for the in-repo code layer, emitting package/symbol producers + import/reference consumers equivalent to the tree-sitter backend. Selected automatically when a mache .db is present; otherwise the T3-2a tree-sitter fallback runs. Surface the honest Available()/sentinel reason when the mache path is requested but unavailable — do NOT fake success or silently degrade.

Acceptance: with a fixture mache .db present, reads v_defs/v_refs and emits package/symbol producers + import consumers equivalent to the tree-sitter backend for the same fixture; the mache-unavailable path returns a clear reason rather than silently degrading; backend selection is deterministic; output deterministic for the same fixture+backend.

title: internal/report: emit map as JSON + Markdown/mermaid (supersedes report.go errcheck in assay-14705d)
description:
[T5-1] Retarget internal/coverage/report.go into internal/report to emit the derived map: machine-readable JSON (delegating to internal/graph serialization) and a human Markdown/mermaid rendering grouped by resolved/external/dangling buckets. Reuse the existing report.go scaffolding. IMPORTANT: this SUPERSEDES the internal/coverage/report.go portion of assay-14705d — fix the pre-existing errcheck on the ~12 fmt.Fprintf calls while moving them here; assay-14705d is rescoped to internal/code/extract.go staticcheck only, so no shared-file conflict. Deterministic output ordering.

Acceptance: internal/report renders a given graph to JSON and to Markdown containing a mermaid diagram with nodes grouped/labeled by bucket; output deterministic for identical input; fmt.Fprintf return values checked (no errcheck warnings in moved code); a golden test asserts exact Markdown+JSON for a fixture graph.

title: cmd: wire `assay map <root...>` end-to-end (derive + emit)
description:
[T5-2] Add the `assay map <root...>` Cobra command: register the four v1 extractors (gomod, gocode via its always-available T3-2a path, dockerfile, ci), run the extract registry over all given roots, run the global resolver, build the graph, emit via internal/report (JSON and/or Markdown by flag). One or more roots; mono-repo and multi-repo handled identically. Full v1 pipeline wired. Depends on the gocode tree-sitter path (T3-2a), NOT the mache .db backend (T3-2b), so the pipeline ships even if T3-2b slips.

Acceptance: `assay map <singleRoot>` emits a map; `assay map <rootA> <rootB>` emits a map whose edges may cross roots; output format selectable by flag; an integration test runs the command over two fixture roots and asserts the emitted map.

title: Motivating cross-root case: golden + integration test
description:
[T5-3] Add the spec's motivating-case test and a golden-graph determinism test. Motivating case: two fixture roots where an artifact built/published in root A is consumed in root B resolves to EXACTLY ONE resolved cross-root edge (modeled on the real x-ray->mache Go-module-require + Docker-source-fetch edge, and/or a fixture image published by CI/Dockerfile in A and pulled/FROM'd in B). Golden test: a fixture root tree -> byte-identical emitted graph on repeated runs (the determinism the LLM seam-discovery skill lacks). Fixtures live in cmd/testdata/crossroot/ (disjoint from T6-1's cmd/testdata/drift/).

Acceptance: an integration test with two fixture roots (producer in A, consumer in B) under cmd/testdata/crossroot/ asserts exactly one resolved cross-root edge labeled resolved (not external); a golden test asserts the full emitted graph is byte-identical across two runs; both run under `task test`.

title: Thin drift fallback: `assay drift <root...> --against <doc>`
description:
[T6-1] GATED by T0-4 (build only if drift is in-v1). Implement the thin fallback grading mode: derive the map (reusing the T5-2 pipeline), then check whether a hand-written ARCHITECTURE.md mentions each resolved seam, reporting the gap. The only surviving descendant of the old coverage/presence metric, demoted to one weak derived view. Reuse internal/report for output. Does NOT change the derive-first arrow. Fixtures live in cmd/testdata/drift/ (disjoint from T5-3's cmd/testdata/crossroot/).

Acceptance: if T0-4 decides in-v1: `assay drift <root> --against ARCHITECTURE.md` reports which resolved seams are/aren't mentioned; a test using cmd/testdata/drift/ asserts a missing-seam fixture is flagged and a documented-seam fixture passes. If T0-4 defers, this bead is parked unbuilt.

title: Document the shipped command surface + extractor→resolver→graph→map diagram
description:
[T7-3] After the pipeline ships, extend CLAUDE.md/README.md with the actual `assay map <root...>` command surface (and `assay drift` if T0-4 ships it), the four v1 extractors, and the architecture diagram reflecting extractors->resolver->graph->map. title: Validate engine against real ecosystem (x-ray→mache, mache→ley-line, rosary image)
description:
[T7-2] Run `assay map` over the real scan roots and confirm the engine surfaces the grounded cross-repo edges: x-ray -> mache (Go module require + Dockerfile source-fetch), mache -> ley-line-open (Go module require, subdir module path), and the rosary ghcr.io/agentic-research/rosary image declaration (publish-side producer). title: assay map — deterministic cross-repo artifact/usage (dependency/seam) graph
description: Reframed assay direction (supersedes the doc-coverage/claims framing of assay-dk6): derive the cross-repo artifact/usage graph from reality (code + build + CI) and emit it as the architecture/seam map (mermaid + JSON). code->docs (derive), not docs->code (grade). Model: Artifact (stable global identity: gomod / gosymbol / image / binary), Edge (producer->consumer), Extractor (deterministic, emits facts with provenance, never matches), Resolver (resolved / external / dangling — external computed, not configured). Headline: the cross-root build edge — an image built in repo A and FROM/CI-pulled in repo B — a seam no single-repo AST tool sees. Deterministic version of the seam-discovery skill. The hand-maintained 'repo dependency graph' mermaid now in art-wiki/README.md is exactly the artifact this is meant to generate. Ref: art-wiki assay.md.

comments.text:
IDENTITY NORMALIZATION decision folded into this vocab bead (no dedicated 9ff bead per lead). Full rules + worked examples: docs/decisions/0002-identity-normalization.md on branch assay-design-decisions (commit e09bf53). Directly informs the Artifact/Identity types here and the resolver (assay-9ff.6) and gomod/dockerfile/ci extractors (9ff.2/9ff.3/9ff.4).

Core model: each artifact kind gets Canonicalize(raw,ctx) producing TWO keys — IdentityKey (version-stripped; the resolver matches on this) and StrictKey (with tag/digest/module-version) — plus OwningRepo and a version_match annotation. Two passes: (1) interpolation (resolve CI ${{...}} from per-root context), then (2) normalization (fold implicit/optional components).

Per kind:
- image: default registry docker.io (bare name -> docker.io/library/name), implicit :latest, digest beats tag; IdentityKey = registry/repository (host lowercased). e.g. ghcr.io/agentic-research/rosary.
- gomod: strip version incl. pseudo-versions (v0.0.0-2023...-hash); KEEP /v2,/v3 major suffix (real identity); replace directive keys on the LEFT (original) path (x-ray's commented `replace ...mache => ../mache` still resolves the edge); SUBDIR MODULE != owning repo -> emit BOTH module-path identity (full path, what `require` matches) AND owning-repo identity (host/owner/repo = first 3 segments). Directly addresses this bead's "Module path != repo identity (subdir modules)" note.
- CI env interpolation: github.repository_owner -> origin owner (agentic-research); env.* resolved from the workflow's own env: block first.

WORKED (grounded in real corpus): ghcr.io/${{github.repository_owner}}/rosary (rosary release.yml:21) -> ghcr.io/agentic-research/rosary, matches a consumer FROM ...:latest as ONE resolved cross-root edge, version_match=mismatch (v0.1.0 vs latest). github.com/agentic-research/mache (mache go.mod) matches x-ray go.mod:6 `require ...mache v0.5.5` after stripping v0.5.5. ley-line-open/clients/go/leyline-schema -> owning repo github.com/agentic-research/ley-line-open. golang:1.26 -> docker.io/library/golang (external); gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12 -> external (x-ray Dockerfile).

Resolver (assay-9ff.6) groups by (Kind,IdentityKey); resolve on IdentityKey to MAXIMIZE seam discovery and surface version mismatches as weak edges rather than misses (the main tunable — flagged for human). Suggested CanonicalID/CIContext struct shapes are in the ADR. (Retargeted here from superseded decade bead assay-926eb8 per lead.) Left open for human review.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

comments.text: Identity-normalization policy decided (T0-3, docs/decisions/0002-identity-normalization.md, branch assay-design-decisions): two-key model — IdentityKey (version-stripped; the resolver matches on this) + StrictKey — produced by an interpolation pass (github.repository_owner->agentic-research, workflow env.*) then a normalization pass. Image: default registry docker.io, default tag :latest, digest>tag, key=registry/repository. Go module: strip version/pseudo-version (keep /vN), replace on original path; a subdir module emits BOTH its module-path identity AND its owning-repo identity. This is the policy the pluggable canonical key in internal/artifact should implement; extractors emit raw refs, normalization layers in front.
comments.author: jamestexas

title: Registry/gomod: one malformed go.mod aborts the whole multi-root scan

title: Decision: mache coupling — read .db/views directly vs MCP/subprocess
description: Open design question from assay.md: does extract/gocode read mache's .db / canonical views (v_defs/v_refs) directly, or talk over MCP/subprocess? Affects whether mache must be running. Decide + record.

comments.text:
DECISION: read mache's .db canonical views DIRECTLY (not MCP/subprocess for the common path). Full ADR: docs/decisions/0001-mache-coupling.md on branch assay-design-decisions (commit e09bf53).

extract/gocode (assay-9ff.5) opens mache's on-disk .db READ-ONLY via the pure-Go modernc.org/sqlite driver (driver name "sqlite", no cgo — same dep mache already uses, go.mod:22 v1.52.0). mache does NOT need to be running. assay gets a .db either supplied or by shelling out once to `mache build <root> <tmp.db>` (a build step, not a live dependency), then sql.Open("sqlite", dbPath+"?mode=ro") and ingest.EnsureCanonicalViews(db) (mache exports CanonicalViewsDDL + EnsureCanonicalViews at sqlite_writer.go:292-328 for exactly this consumer case, so older .dbs without the views still work; assay MAY inline the idempotent CREATE VIEW IF NOT EXISTS DDL instead of importing mache).

Read surface (ADR-0013, sqlite_writer.go:118-128):
- v_defs(token, node_id, fidelity) — symbol definitions
- v_refs(referrer_node_id, token, target_node_id, ref_uri, ref_line, fidelity) — references
Join nodes(id,name,kind,source_file) + _ast(node_id,source_id,start_row) for file:line provenance. fidelity is 'mention' today; when LSP binding rows land they UNION ALL in and consumer SQL is unchanged (ADR-0013 forward-compat — assay's edges get more precise for free).

stdio/MCP is FALLBACK ONLY: no `mache` binary on PATH, or un-parsed live sources where rebuilding isn't acceptable. Otherwise fall back to in-tree tree-sitter (internal/code) per assay-9ff.5's stated Available()+sentinel pattern.

Grounded by reading mache: go.mod:22, sqlite_writer.go:118-128/:248 (read-only LoadFileIndex pattern assay mirrors)/:292-328, ADR-0013. Residual risk: v_defs/v_refs are ADR-0013 "Proposed" — pin a fixture .db + golden test; pin `mache build --backend=tree-sitter` for deterministic byte ranges. (Retargeted here from superseded decade bead assay-925a0d per lead.) Left open for human review.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

comments.text: DECISION (T0-1, from branch assay-design-decisions commit e09bf53, docs/decisions/0001-mache-coupling.md): extract/gocode reads mache's canonical views v_defs/v_refs READ-ONLY via pure-Go modernc.org/sqlite (driver "sqlite", no cgo). mache need NOT be running. The .db is supplied or built once via `mache build <root> <tmp.db>`; call mache's exported EnsureCanonicalViews to tolerate older .dbs; join nodes/_ast for file:line provenance. stdio/MCP is fallback only for un-parsed live sources. Grounded: mache go.mod:22, internal/ingest/sqlite_writer.go:118-128/248/292-328, ADR-0013. => gocode (9ff.5) builds against this.

title: Consolidate identity normalization into internal/artifact (decision-0002 CanonicalID)
description: Surfaced at integration by the 9ff.6 resolver. Decision-0002 (docs/decisions/0002-identity-normalization.md) specified a CanonicalID / IdentityKey (version-stripped) + StrictKey living in internal/artifact as the single source of truth. It was NOT implemented there — so internal/resolve/buckets.go re-derives identity+version from Identity.Ref ad hoc (go_module split on @ keeping /vN; image strip @digest/:tag after final /), and the extractors emit raw Refs. This works ONLY while both sides parse refs identically — a latent consistency bug. Fix: implement CanonicalID/IdentityKey in internal/artifact per decision-0002; have extractors stamp it and the resolver consume precomputed keys instead of re-parsing. Priority/severity to be confirmed by the 9ff.7 real-ecosystem run (if the x-ray->mache edge resolves, this is cleanup; if not, it's a blocker).

title: Language-agnostic extraction: consume ley-line `.db` (_ast/nodes) instead of Go-only smacker/go-tree-sitter
description:

Written up as the explicit OPEN follow-up in mache-1c332f: "whether assay's deterministic graph consumes ley-line's .db/arena rather than its own CGO tree-sitter."

title: Security: .beads/.beads-credential-key is not gitignored
description: The bead credential key file .beads/.beads-credential-key is untracked but NOT covered by any .gitignore rule (.beads/.gitignore ignores dolt/, sockets, sync-state, .local_version, etc. but not the credential key).
